data.title: SiteOne Landscape Supply 10-Q: $940.1M Revenue, $(0.60) EPS
data.description:
SiteOne Landscape Supply reported quarterly net sales of $940.1 million and a net loss per share of $(0.60), reflecting a net loss attributable to the company of $(26.6) million. Revenue was essentially flat year‑over‑year with organic daily sales down 1%, while gross margin expanded to 33.9% driven by price realization. Operating loss narrowed versus the prior year as higher gross profit partially offset ongoing cost pressures.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $940.1 million (Organic Daily Sales down 1% y/y; acquisitions contributed $12.4 million)
  • Gross profit: $318.8 million; gross margin 33.9% (up 90 basis points from prior year)
  • Operating loss: $(25.9) million (improved from $(29.5) million prior year)
  • Net loss: $(24.1) million (Net loss attributable to SiteOne $(26.6) million after non‑controlling interest)
  • Net loss per common share (Basic & Diluted): $(0.60); weighted average shares 44,586,582

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Net sales were flat year‑over‑year; organic daily sales declined 1% due to adverse weather and softer new residential demand.
  • Pricing and margin actions: Continued price realization (approx. 2–3% guidance) helped drive a 90 basis point improvement in gross margin despite higher freight costs.
  • Channel and product mix: Agronomics segment grew roughly 2% driven by pricing; landscaping products declined about 3% linked to weather and weaker construction activity.
  • Acquisitions and network expansion: Completed multiple tuck‑in acquisitions (including Reinders, Bourget, French Broad and others) and opened a fifth distribution center to expand footprint.
  • Operational initiatives: Ongoing focus on supply chain optimization, category management, digital/B2B e‑commerce enhancements, distribution optimization, and improving branch productivity.

Original SEC Filing: SiteOne Landscape Supply, Inc. [ SITE ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Rogers 10-Q: Q1 sales $200.5M; EPS $0.25
data.description:
Rogers reported first-quarter net sales of $200.5 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.25, driven by higher sales in industrial and electronics end markets and foreign exchange benefits; improved margins and cost actions helped lift net income to $4.5 million. The quarter included $5.9 million of restructuring and impairment charges related to a Germany consolidation and leadership changes. Cash remained roughly $196 million with planned capital spending of $30–40 million for 2026 capacity and R&D.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $200.5 million, up 5.2% year-over-year (FX benefit of $7.9 million).
  • Gross margin: $64.6 million, 32.2% of sales, an increase of 230 basis points versus prior year.
  • Operating income (loss): $10.7 million, representing 5.3% of sales; includes $5.9 million of restructuring/impairment charges.
  • Net income (loss): $4.5 million, or 2.2% of sales, compared with a loss in the prior-year period.
  • Diluted net income per share: $0.25, versus $(0.08) in the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Higher demand in industrial and electronics end markets supported overall sales growth; automotive demand softened.
  • Segment performance: AES rose 3.4% and EMS increased 7.0%, reflecting strength in electronics and industrial channels.
  • Operational efficiency: Margin improvement credited to higher volumes, a favorable product mix and consolidation of the Belgium footprint.
  • Cost and workforce actions: Announced restructuring tied to consolidating operations in Germany, along with leadership transitions and workforce reductions.
  • Liquidity and investment outlook: Cash balance remained near $196 million; capital expenditures planned at $30–40 million in 2026 to support capacity expansion and R&D initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: ROGERS CORP [ ROG ] - 10-Q - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Wilmar's first-quarter profit slides on hedging losses, weaker associate incomes
data.description:
Singapore-listed Wilmar International reported a 23% fall in first-quarter core net profit on Wednesday, primarily due to hedging losses from market volatility linked to the Iran conflict and weaker contributions from associates.

The company said it expects these losses to reverse in the coming quarters, when physical transactions are settled.

Meanwhile, its associates and joint ventures across China, Europe and Southeast Asia regions made weaker contributions during the quarter, Wilmar said.

The food conglomerate said profits from its plantation and sugar milling segment also declined due to lower palm oil prices and production volume, as well as weaker sugar performance.

Wilmar posted a core net profit of $264.2 million for the three months ended March 31, down from $343 million a year earlier.

data.title: Diesel market structure eases further; front-month east-west spreads widen
data.description:
Asia's diesel markets eased again for a second straight session, with paper swaps easing into a narrower backwardation and cash differentials softening to a nearly two-month low, despite persistently thin spot activity on the trading window.

China's exports are still likely to be curbed for next month, with limited diesel volumes - probably below 100,000 tons - expected to regional destinations, traders say.

June spot sales could start emerging in the next week from other regional exporters, though discussion levels could ease further given the buyer unwillingness to pay higher prices for now.

Front month east-west price spreads were at larger discounts of $90-100 per metric ton, their widest since a week earlier.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) regained some ground slightly, recovering to a two-session high of around $58 a barrel.

Lower offers on the window weighed on 10ppm sulphur gasoil cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF), with values easing further to around $10.5 a barrel - though these levels were still higher than pre-conflict.

Jet fuel exports from China for next month will still mostly be to Hong Kong.

Meanwhile arbitrage price spreads between Asia and the U.S. west coast remained wide, at discounts of more than $15 a barrel, with freight costs at around $8 a barrel.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) inched down further to premiums of $5 a barrel.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No deal for gasoil or jet fuel

INVENTORIES

- U.S. crude oil inventories fell for the second straight week, while gasoline and distillate stocks also declined, market sources said, citing American Petroleum Institute figures on Tuesday.

- Stocks for refined oil products at Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates have fallen to fresh lows of less than 7 million barrels, data showed on Wednesday, after the Iran war disrupted energy supplies in the region.

REFINERY NEWS

- India's Nayara Energy is expected to resume operations by mid-May at its Vadinar refinery in western Gujarat state after a maintenance shutdown, said Sujata Sharma, a joint secretary in the federal oil ministry.

NEWS

- China has approved 500,000 metric tons of fuel exports for May to regions other than Hong Kong, representing a near doubling of shipments forecast for April, trading sources said, but levels remain less than half of last year's corresponding averages.

- China's Sinopec on Wednesday said its refinery and petrochemical utilisation rates dropped in the first quarter as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran disrupted feedstock supplies, but its chemical exports are set for strong growth this year.

- OPEC and its allies will lose some of their power over the oil market when the United Arab Emirates leaves the group on May 1, but the rest of the producer alliance is likely to stick together and continue to coordinate on oil supply policy, OPEC+ delegates and analysts said on Tuesday.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.79% at 77,496.36, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index advanced 0.76% to 24,177.65, as stock-specific gains after quarterly earnings offset higher Brent crude prices on reports the U.S. may extend its blockade of Iranian ports.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.3% versus the U.S. dollar to 94.850, pressured by another rise in oil prices as efforts to end the Iran conflict stalled and foreign selling of Indian assets persisted.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 96.47 rupees, with the yield up 1.43 bps at 6.998%.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 3 basis points at 5.99%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 3.75 bps to 6.63%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.20% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.17%.

data.title: Bunge beats Q1 adj. EPS estimates, lifts full-year outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • Agribusiness and food ingredients firm's Q1 revenue missed analyst expectations, adjusted EPS beat consensus

  • Company raised full-year adjusted EPS outlook, citing improved processing and refining performance

  • Higher results driven by strong execution and improved market conditions, especially in South America and North America

Outlook

  • Bunge raises full-year 2026 adjusted EPS outlook to $9.00-$9.50 from $7.50-$8.00

  • Company expects higher 2026 results in Soybean and Softseed Processing, lower in Tropical Oils and Grain

  • Bunge sees 2026 adjusted annual tax rate at 22%-26% and net interest expense at $620-$660 mln

Result Drivers

  • SOYBEAN & SOFTSEED PROCESSING - Higher results driven by expanded production capacity and increased origination in South America and North America, per company

  • SOFTSEED VOLUMES & FOOTPRINT - Increased softseed processed and merchandised volumes due to expanded origination footprint and large crops in Canada and Australia, per company

  • OILS MERCHANDISING EXECUTION - Results from global oils merchandising activities increased, reflecting strong execution, per company

data.title: Middle East Crude-Dubai, Oman premiums fall; Murban steady
data.description:
The Middle East crude benchmark premium for Dubai fell to its lowest in nearly two months on Wednesday, while Oman's premium also declined and Murban held steady.

In recent weeks, spot premiums for physical crude have slipped from record highs hit earlier in the Iran war as refiners are drawing on inventories and cutting back processing to cope with disrupted Middle East supply.

However, some traders expect premiums to rebound if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked and supply remains tight.

Oil prices rose nearly 3% on Wednesday, with the Brent crude contract hitting a one-month high, on media reports that the U.S. will extend its blockade of Iranian ports, likely prolonging supply disruptions from the key Middle East producing region.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps fell $3.89 to $7.41 a barrel.

Shell will deliver a June-loading Oman crude cargo to TotalEnergies following the deals.

data.title: ZAWYA: Vertiv strengthens liquid-cooling system capability with acquisition of strategic thermal labs
data.description:
Dubai, UAE,  Vertiv , a global leader in critical digital infrastructure and continuity solutions, today announced it has acquired Strategic Thermal Labs LLC (STL), a specialist in advanced liquid-cooling technologies. The acquisition extends Vertiv’s thermal-chain strategy by strengthening engineering capability at the interface between server-side liquid cooling and supporting infrastructure—an increasingly critical factor in high-density, liquid-cooled environments supporting AI and high-performance computing workloads.

As compute requirements continue to intensify, the interaction between server-side liquid cooling and supporting infrastructure increasingly influences broader system performance, including flow, balance, controls behavior, serviceability, and lifecycle reliability. Strategic Thermal Labs adds proven cold-plate design, server-side liquid cooling, and high-density thermal validation expertise that is expected to strengthen Vertiv’s ability to simulate and emulate real high-density compute conditions, optimize the interaction between the thermal chain and power train, and support customers across design, integration, commissioning, and lifecycle operations.

“As AI and high‑performance computing push power densities to unprecedented levels, understanding and solving heat challenges at the chip level becomes critical to system design, performance and reliability,” said Scott Armul, chief product and technology officer at Vertiv. “STL brings deep expertise and proven capability in addressing some of the industry’s most demanding chip-level density and thermal problems, strengthening Vertiv’s ability to emulate and validate system-level solutions and enabling customers to improve performance and lifecycle outcomes in liquid-cooled environments.”

Vertiv emphasized that the acquisition does not change its commitment to an open ecosystem approach. The company will continue to support interoperable, server‑ and silicon‑agnostic infrastructure solutions, with the goal of improving system‑level performance and customer outcomes across diverse compute environments.

The addition of Strategic Thermal Labs supports Vertiv’s broader strategy of helping customers address increasing infrastructure complexity through integrated power, thermal, controls, and lifecycle services capabilities.

For more information about Vertiv’s end‑to‑end portfolio of power and thermal management systems, infrastructures solutions, and lifecycle services, visit Vertiv.com.

About Vertiv

Vertiv brings together hardware, software, analytics and ongoing services to enable its customers’ vital applications to run continuously, perform optimally and grow with their business needs. Vertiv solves the most important challenges facing today’s data centers, communication networks and commercial and industrial facilities with a portfolio of power, cooling and IT infrastructure solutions and services that extends from the cloud to the edge of the network. Headquartered in Westerville, Ohio, USA, Vertiv does business in more than 130 countries. For more information, and for the latest news and content from Vertiv, visit Vertiv.com.

data.title: Amadeus to buy French biometrics firm Idemia Public Security for $1.4 billion
data.description:
Spanish travel technology firm Amadeus on Wednesday announced a plan to acquire French biometrics company Idemia Public Security, which is owned by Advent International, for 1.2 billion euros ($1.40 billion) in an all-cash deal.

Amadeus, which operates the world's largest travel booking system, described the acquisition as immediately earnings-accretive, expecting it to accelerate its growth in airports and border checkpoints while enhancing the firm's global presence.

The combination of the two businesses should increase Amadeus' total addressable market, or total revenue opportunity, to 50 billion euros from 41 billion, it said.

"Potentially a good deal, and a fit with Amadeus' portfolio," Bernstein analysts said in a note to investors, adding that the acquisition will likely reduce the probability of share buybacks for the second half of the year.

Amadeus shares were down 0.65% at 48.9 euros in morning trading on Wednesday.

The closing of the deal is expected in mid-2027 and it includes a potential earn-out of up to 150 million euros on top of the price.

Amadeus expected the acquisition to result in high single-digit revenue growth with expanding operating margins, and provide annual cost synergies of 50 million euros in the medium term.

IPS employs around 3,300 people worldwide and serves more than 600 public and private sector customers. Aside from passenger processing, IPS works with other regulated environments such as access control and government-grade biometric identification and data solutions.

($1 = 0.8548 euros)

data.title: Rupee hits record closing low hurt by surging oil, persistent outflows
data.description:
By Jaspreet Kalra

The Indian rupee fell to a record closing low on Wednesday, pressured by rising oil prices as efforts to end the Iran conflict remained stalled, while persistent foreign selling of Indian assets added to the strain.

The rupee closed at 94.8450 per dollar, down 0.3% on the day.

The currency has drifted back toward record lows as the impact of central banks' rupee supportive measures faded, with concerns over India's exposure to higher energy prices weighing on sentiment.

Dollar sales by state-run banks, likely on behalf of the RBI, helped limit losses, traders said.

Brent crude rose more than 3% to nearly $115 per barrel, weighing on other oil-sensitive Asian currencies such as the Philippine peso and Indonesian rupiah, both of which hit record lows on Wednesday.

U.S. President Donald Trump has instructed aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran, the Wall Street Journal reported late on Tuesday, citing U.S. officials.

Analysts and traders expect the rupee to remain under strain as long as oil prices remain elevated but a fall below 95 could draw sterner interventions by the Reserve Bank of India.

"Around these levels last month the RBI had stepped in heavily with both dollar sales and regulatory measures, at least one of those could be used again," a trader at a state-run bank said.

Global markets, meanwhile, are also awaiting the U.S. Federal Reserve's policy decision. No change in benchmark rates is expected but investors will pay close attention to commentary on how the Iran war will impact the world's largest economy.

"We think it makes sense to brace for a higher-for-longer rates world, amidst what looks to be higher-for-longer inflation," analysts at DBS said in a note.

Fed funds futures are pricing in no policy changes by the Fed until late in 2027, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool.

data.title: Indian shares advance as earnings optimism counters oil surge
data.description:
By Bharath Rajeswaran

Indian shares advanced on Wednesday as stock-specific gains after quarterly earnings offset higher Brent crude prices on reports the U.S. may extend its blockade of Iranian ports, limiting the upside.

Brent crude hovered around $115 a barrel. Higher oil prices pose risks for India, the world's third-largest crude importer, by fuelling inflation and pressuring growth and corporate earnings.

The Nifty 50 rose 0.76% at 24,177.65, and Sensex added 0.79% to 77,496.36.

Eleven of the 16 major sectors advanced. The auto index rose 1.2%.

Maruti Suzuki jumped 2.9%, rebounding from Tuesday's 2.5% drop after its surprise March-quarter profit fall as multiple brokerages pointed to steady demand and volumes as positives.

Index heavyweight Reliance gained 2.6% and took its three-session rise to 7.4% after quarterly results .

Vedanta rose 4.6% after posting higher quarter profit on base metal price surge.

Eternal , Bandhan Bank , Star Health , Dalmia Bharat and Canara HSBC Life surged 0.3%-11% on upbeat quarterly results.

The broader small-caps rose 0.7% while mid-caps were little changed.

"By and large, the earnings season has had no negative surprises, except for the subdued growth guidance by IT companies, which is supporting sentiment," said Kranthi Bathini, director of equity strategy at WealthMills Securities.

While crude prices have risen and foreign outflows from Indian equities already exceeded total sales in 2025, markets have swung between gains and losses in recent sessions, as attractive valuations, strong domestic buying and bargain hunting provide a counterweight, Bathini said.

Meanwhile, markets await the Federal Reserve's rate decision, due after market hours on Wednesday. While a pause is priced in, the commentary on inflation could offer clues into future rate trajectory.

data.title: Humana beats quarterly profit estimates on lower medical costs
data.description:
Humana on Wednesday beat Wall Street estimates for first-quarter profit as the health insurer spent less on medical services than expected.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned a profit of $10.31 per share, higher than analysts' estimates of $10.19 per share, per data compiled by LSEG.
